betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Station Gravity Controller§f is a §6single block§f placed on a space station to provide §6artificial gravity§f in a configurable radius. Without it, the station is a §60-G vacuum§f and players must use jetpacks or the §6Space Suit§f's jet boots to navigate. The controller is one of the §6three core station infrastructure blocks§f (alongside the docking port and warp controller).

§6Production Path (gravitycontroller.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shapeless§f
- Input: 1x §6Machine Structure§f (libvulpes:structuremachine), 1x §6Piston§f (minecraft:piston), 1x §6Block of Redstone§f (minecraft:redstone_block)
- Output: 1x §6Station Gravity Controller§f (advancedrocketry:gravitycontroller)

The controller creates a spherical gravity field of up to §664 blocks§f radius (configurable in the GUI). It requires §6RF power§f to maintain the field — 100 RF/t baseline plus 1 RF/t per block of radius. Multiple controllers can be linked to extend the field to larger stations.

The gravity controller is a prerequisite for the §6Warp Travel§f (10038) chain and is required by every station that hosts §6passenger missions§f. It also enables the §6Area Gravity Controller§f (chain) for non-station use.

§eTip§f: The §6Block of Redstone§f in the recipe provides the §6default gravity strength§f (9.81 m/s²). Replace it with §6Block of Redstone × N§f to scale the strength linearly — a stack of 64 produces 64× Earth gravity for very heavy ore processing stations.
